top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 82, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Rock Cobbler's Central Valley gravel, Rocky sections forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 82 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Rock Cobbler's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before February.
106ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Rock Cobbler, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Rock Cobbler. Train it until it feels boring.
8,700ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Rock Cobbler, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 82 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
8,700ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on central Valley gravel and dirt—rocks, rollers. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
The aerobic base that lets you ride central Valley gravel and dirt—rocks, rollers at tempo for 82 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.heat: February in Bakersfield, California can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Rock Cobbler. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Rock Cobbler’s 82 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Rock Cobbler requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on central valley gravel and dirt—rocks, rollers to build confidence. Expect: central valley gravel, rocky sections, dirt roads.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 82 miles of central valley gravel and dirt—rocks, rollers in Bakersfield, California.
